q: An inertial frame is a frame of reference that has no acceleration.
The term "radioactivity" is used to describe the rate at which a radioactive sample undergoes decay.
Gamma rays can be deflected by electric and magnetic fields.
A nuclear reaction is a process in which a beta radiation penetrates into a nucleus and changes its configuration.
In $\beta^-$ decay, the atomic number of daughter nucleus increases by one.
Activity of a radioactive sample decreases with time.
Relativistic effect usually becomes negligible when speed of the system becomes comparable to the speed of light.
The wave nature of electrons is used instead of light in electron microscope.
All elements in nature are not radioactive.
Alpha particles consist of two protons and neutrons making them identical to a helium nucleus.
An inertial frame is a frame of reference that has no acceleration.
Beta particles are high-energy electrons or positrons emitted during radioactive decay.
Positron is also called the antiparticle of proton.
Gamma rays have no charge.
In $\beta^-$ decay, the atomic number of daughter nucleus increases by one.
Alpha rays can be deflected by electric and magnetic fields.
Shielding is a protective barrier, usually a dense material.
Beta rays are less penetrating than alpha rays.
Neutrons released from a fission reaction are slow neutrons.
Gamma rays can be stopped by a thick sheet of paper.
The Michelson Morley experiment provide evidence for the existence of the ether.
